chunks.title: Elkanah's Family and Hannah's Distress
chunks.reason: Introduces the family background and the conflict between Hannah and Peninnah.

chunks.title: Hannah's Prayer and Eli's Response
chunks.reason: Depicts Hannah's silent prayer, her misunderstanding by Eli, and his blessing.

chunks.title: Birth of Samuel and the Vow Fulfilled
chunks.reason: Narrates the conception, birth, naming of Samuel, and Hannah's decision to wait until weaning.

chunks.title: Presentation of Samuel at the Temple
chunks.reason: Describes Hannah bringing Samuel to Shiloh and dedicating him to Yahweh.

chunks.title: Hannah's Song of Praise
chunks.reason: A complete poetic prayer celebrating God's sovereignty and justice.

chunks.title: Samuel's Early Ministry
chunks.reason: Narrative covering Samuel's service, Eli's sons' corruption, and Hannah's subsequent children.

chunks.title: Prophecy Against Eli
chunks.reason: A formal divine judgment delivered by a man of God against the house of Eli.

chunks.title: Samuel's First Call
chunks.reason: Describes the initial sequence of Samuel hearing God's voice and mistaking it for Eli's call.

chunks.title: Oracle Against Eli
chunks.reason: Contains the specific divine judgment pronounced against Eli and his house.

chunks.title: Samuel Reports to Eli
chunks.reason: Depicts Samuel's hesitation and eventual disclosure of the vision to Eli.

chunks.title: Samuel Established as Prophet
chunks.reason: Summarizes Samuel's growth and the public recognition of his prophetic ministry.

chunks.title: Israel Defeated and the Ark Taken to Camp
chunks.reason: Describes the initial battle loss and the decision to bring the ark from Shiloh.

chunks.title: The Second Battle and Ark Captured
chunks.reason: Covers the Philistines' reaction to the ark's arrival, the second defeat, and the capture of the ark.

chunks.title: The Messenger Arrives at Shiloh
chunks.reason: Narrates the runner's arrival and Eli's death upon hearing the news.

chunks.title: Death of Phinehas's Wife and Naming of Ichabod
chunks.reason: Details the wife's death in childbirth and her naming of the son to signify the departure of glory.

chunks.title: Dagon Falls Before the Ark in Ashdod
chunks.reason: Describes the arrival of the ark in Ashdod and the repeated humiliation of the god Dagon.

chunks.title: The Plague of Tumors in Ashdod
chunks.reason: Details the divine judgment on the city and the Philistines' decision to move the ark.

chunks.title: The Ark Moves to Gath
chunks.reason: Records the transfer of the ark to Gath and the subsequent plague there.

chunks.title: The Ark Moves to Ekron
chunks.reason: Describes the panic in Ekron and the final decision to send the ark away.

chunks.title: Philistine Counsel on the Ark
chunks.reason: The Philistines consult priests and diviners to determine how to return the ark with a guilt offering.

chunks.title: Return of the Ark to Beth-shemesh
chunks.reason: Narrative of the Philistines sending the ark on a cart and its arrival at Beth-shemesh.

chunks.title: List of Guilt Offerings
chunks.reason: Catalog of the five golden tumors and mice corresponding to the Philistine cities.

chunks.title: Judgment at Beth-shemesh and Transfer
chunks.reason: The people of Beth-shemesh are struck for looking into the ark and send messengers to Kiriath-jearim.

chunks.title: The Ark at Kiriath-jearim
chunks.reason: Narrative account of the ark's placement and the twenty-year period of Israel's lament.

chunks.title: Call to Repentance at Mizpah
chunks.reason: Samuel's exhortation to remove idols followed by the people's gathering and confession.

chunks.title: Victory over the Philistines
chunks.reason: The battle narrative where God intervenes through thunder and Israel pursues the enemy.

chunks.title: Samuel's Annual Circuit
chunks.reason: Summary of Samuel's judicial ministry and his return to Ramah.

chunks.title: Samuel's Sons and the Elders' Request
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction of Samuel's aging sons and the elders' demand for a king.

chunks.title: Samuel Prays and Yahweh Responds
chunks.reason: Dialogue between Samuel and God regarding the people's rejection of divine kingship.

chunks.title: The Custom of the King
chunks.reason: Samuel delivers a detailed speech listing the burdens and rights of a future king.

chunks.title: The People's Refusal
chunks.reason: The elders insist on having a king despite Samuel's warning.

chunks.title: Final Appointment of the King
chunks.reason: Samuel reports to God and receives final instructions to appoint a king.

chunks.title: Genealogy of Kish and Saul
chunks.reason: Introduces the lineage and physical description of Saul's family.

chunks.title: Saul Searches for Donkeys
chunks.reason: Narrative of the search and discovery of the seer.

chunks.title: Samuel Meets Saul
chunks.reason: Combines divine oracle with dialogue between Samuel and Saul.

chunks.title: The Banquet at the High Place
chunks.reason: Describes the meal where Samuel honors Saul.

chunks.title: Evening Conversation and Preparation
chunks.reason: Narrative of the evening talk and early morning departure.

chunks.title: Anointing and Signs for Saul
chunks.reason: Samuel anoints Saul and gives him prophetic signs to confirm his calling.

chunks.title: Saul Prophesies Among the Prophets
chunks.reason: The narrative describes Saul's transformation and the people's reaction.

chunks.title: Saul Hides the Kingdom from His Uncle
chunks.reason: A brief conversation where Saul conceals his anointing from his uncle.

chunks.title: Selection of Saul at Mizpah
chunks.reason: Samuel gathers the tribes to select Saul as king through a lot.

chunks.title: Establishment of the Kingdom and Opposition
chunks.reason: Samuel establishes legal judgments while Saul faces initial opposition from vile men.

chunks.title: Nahash besieges Jabesh-gilead
chunks.reason: The Ammonite siege and the elders' plea for a seven-day delay.

chunks.title: Saul's call to arms
chunks.reason: Saul receives the news, is filled with the Spirit, and gathers the army.

chunks.title: The battle against Ammon
chunks.reason: The messengers deliver Saul's ultimatum and the decisive victory.

chunks.title: Saul confirmed as king
chunks.reason: The people demand execution of doubters, Saul shows mercy, and the kingdom is renewed at Gilgal.

chunks.title: Samuel's Self-Defense
chunks.reason: A dialogue where Samuel challenges the people to testify against his integrity and they confirm it.

chunks.title: Historical Review and Covenant Warning
chunks.reason: Samuel recounts Israel's history of deliverance and warns them about the consequences of obeying or rebelling against God.

chunks.title: The Sign of Thunder and Rain
chunks.reason: Samuel calls for a sign to prove God's displeasure, and Yahweh sends thunder and rain.

chunks.title: Final Charge to the People
chunks.reason: The people ask for prayer, and Samuel exhorts them to serve God faithfully while promising his continued intercession.

chunks.title: Saul's Army and the Philistine Threat
chunks.reason: Establishes Saul's reign, military deployment, and the gathering of the Philistine army.

chunks.title: Saul's Sacrifice and Samuel's Rebuke
chunks.reason: Contains the narrative of Saul waiting for Samuel, his impulsive sacrifice, and Samuel's prophetic judgment.

chunks.title: The Scattered Army and the Blacksmiths
chunks.reason: Describes the aftermath of Samuel's departure, the strategic positioning of forces, and the scarcity of weapons among the Israelites.

chunks.title: Jonathan's Victory
chunks.reason: Describes Jonathan's surprise attack on the Philistine garrison and the resulting confusion in their camp.

chunks.title: The Oath and the Honey
chunks.reason: Details Saul's rash oath, Jonathan's tasting of honey, and the people eating meat with blood.

chunks.title: Saul's Oath on Jonathan
chunks.reason: Narrates Saul seeking God's will, casting lots to find the sinner, and the people saving Jonathan.

chunks.title: Saul's Reign Summary
chunks.reason: Summarizes Saul's military campaigns, family members, and his method of recruiting soldiers.

chunks.title: The Divine Command to Saul
chunks.reason: Samuel delivers the specific oracle from Yahweh regarding the war against Amalek.

chunks.title: Saul's Campaign and Partial Victory
chunks.reason: Narrative of Saul gathering troops, defeating Amalek, sparing Agag, and keeping the best livestock.

chunks.title: Samuel's Grief and Saul's Arrival
chunks.reason: Yahweh informs Samuel of Saul's failure, Samuel prays, and Saul sets up a monument at Carmel.

chunks.title: Confrontation over the Spoil
chunks.reason: Saul claims obedience while Samuel questions the noise of livestock and Saul blames the people.

chunks.title: The Rejection of Kingship
chunks.reason: Samuel declares obedience superior to sacrifice, pronounces judgment, and Saul pleads for honor.

chunks.title: Execution of Agag and Final Separation
chunks.reason: Samuel kills Agag, returns to Ramah, and the narrative concludes with their permanent separation.

chunks.title: Samuel Anoints David
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit describing God's instruction to Samuel, the selection process among Jesse's sons, and the anointing of David.

chunks.title: David Enters Saul's Service
chunks.reason: A continuous narrative detailing the departure of God's Spirit from Saul, the search for a musician, and David's introduction to the court.

chunks.title: The Philistine and Israelian Camps
chunks.reason: Describes the positioning of armies and Goliath's challenge.

chunks.title: David Sent to the Camp
chunks.reason: Introduces David and his mission to bring supplies.

chunks.title: David Arrives at the Battle Line
chunks.reason: David arrives, sees Goliath, and inquires about rewards.

chunks.title: Eliab Rebukes David
chunks.reason: David's brother questions his motives before Saul is notified.

chunks.title: David Volunteers to Fight
chunks.reason: David speaks to Saul and recounts his past victories.

chunks.title: David Prepares for Battle
chunks.reason: Saul clothes David, who then chooses his own weapons.

chunks.title: Confrontation with Goliath
chunks.reason: Goliath and David exchange words before the fight.

chunks.title: David Defeats Goliath
chunks.reason: The battle occurs and the Philistines flee.

chunks.title: Saul Questions David's Identity
chunks.reason: Saul asks Abner and David about his lineage.

chunks.title: Jonathan and David's Covenant
chunks.reason: Describes the deep bond between Jonathan and David and Saul's initial acceptance of David.

chunks.title: Saul's Jealousy and Anger
chunks.reason: Details the women's song comparing Saul and David, leading to Saul's rage and first attempt to kill David.

chunks.title: David's Rise and Saul's Suspicion
chunks.reason: Describes David's promotion to commander and Saul's growing dread as David prospers.

chunks.title: The First Marriage Proposal
chunks.reason: Contains the dialogue regarding Saul offering Merab to David and David's humble response.

chunks.title: The Dowry of Foreskins
chunks.reason: Narrates Saul's plan to use the Philistines to kill David through the dowry requirement and its fulfillment.

chunks.title: David's Continued Success
chunks.reason: Concludes with David's superior performance against the Philistines and his high esteem among the people.

chunks.title: Jonathan warns David
chunks.reason: Jonathan intercedes with Saul and relays the warning to David.

chunks.title: David's victory and Saul's attack
chunks.reason: David defeats the Philistines and Saul attempts to kill him with a spear.

chunks.title: Michal's escape plan
chunks.reason: Michal helps David flee and deceives Saul's messengers.

chunks.title: Saul prophesies at Ramah
chunks.reason: Saul sends messengers who prophesy, followed by Saul himself prophesying.

chunks.title: David and Jonathan's Covenant
chunks.reason: A continuous conversation where David seeks confirmation of Saul's intent and they establish a covenant with oaths.

chunks.title: The Signal Plan
chunks.reason: Jonathan details the specific signal system using arrows to communicate David's fate.

chunks.title: The Banquet and Saul's Anger
chunks.reason: Narrative of the new moon feast where David is absent, leading to Saul's confrontation with Jonathan.

chunks.title: The Signal and Farewell
chunks.reason: Jonathan executes the arrow signal to David and they share a final emotional farewell.

chunks.title: David at Nob
chunks.reason: A continuous dialogue between David and Ahimelech involving the exchange of bread and Goliath's sword.

chunks.title: David at Gath
chunks.reason: A narrative sequence describing David's flight to Gath and his feigned madness before King Achish.

chunks.title: David gathers followers and seeks refuge
chunks.reason: Narrative of David moving to Adullam and Mizpah with his family.

chunks.title: Saul discovers David's location
chunks.reason: Saul learns of David and Doeg reports the visit to Nob.

chunks.title: Saul interrogates Ahimelech
chunks.reason: Dialogue between Saul and the priest followed by the king's judgment.

chunks.title: Massacre at Nob and David's comfort
chunks.reason: Doeg kills the priests and Abiathar reports to David.

chunks.title: David Saves Keilah
chunks.reason: A complete narrative unit where David seeks God's direction and delivers the city of Keilah from the Philistines.

chunks.title: David Escapes Saul at Keilah
chunks.reason: A continuous narrative block detailing the arrival of Abiathar, Saul's pursuit, David's inquiry about the city's loyalty, and their subsequent departure.

chunks.title: Jonathan Strengthens David
chunks.reason: A distinct scene focusing on the relationship between Jonathan and David, including their covenant at Horesh.

chunks.title: The Ziphites Betray David
chunks.reason: A focused exchange between the Ziphites and Saul regarding David's location, followed by their movement to Maon.

chunks.title: The Rock of Escape
chunks.reason: The climax of the chase where David and Saul encircle each other on a mountain, interrupted by the Philistine raid.

chunks.title: David Spares Saul in the Cave
chunks.reason: Narrative of Saul entering the cave and David cutting his robe without killing him.

chunks.title: David's Speech to Saul
chunks.reason: David confronts Saul, explains his mercy, and contrasts their positions.

chunks.title: Saul's Repentance and Oath
chunks.reason: Saul weeps, acknowledges David's righteousness, and they make a covenant.

chunks.title: Death of Samuel and David in the Wilderness
chunks.reason: Narrative introduction setting the scene for the main story.

chunks.title: David's Request and Nabal's Rejection
chunks.reason: Complete narrative unit covering the initial interaction and David's angry reaction.

chunks.title: Abigail's Intervention
chunks.reason: The turning point where Abigail learns of the crisis and prepares provisions.

chunks.title: Abigail Meets David and Pleads for Mercy
chunks.reason: The core dramatic encounter featuring Abigail's speech and David's acceptance.

chunks.title: Nabal's Death and Abigail Marries David
chunks.reason: Resolution of the conflict and the conclusion of the narrative arc.
